title: Exertional Chest Pain in the Emergency Department
stem: A 58-year-old man presents with 40 minutes of substernal chest pressure that began while climbing stairs. The pain radiates to his left arm and is associated with nausea and diaphoresis. He has hypertension and smokes.
expected_reasoning_steps: Request immediate ECG and serial troponins.
Keep life-threatening chest pain causes in the differential.
Recommend initial ACS management and monitored disposition.
